overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to disinformation, computational social science, and information operations. Total followers across all platforms amount to 3580, indicating a substantial reach within the academic and professional community. Recent activity reflects a focus on disseminating research findings, announcing job opportunities, and promoting conference presentations.

contentThemes.description: Sharing research papers, findings, and project updates.
Announcing postdoctoral and PhD positions within the research team.
Promoting upcoming presentations and participation in academic conferences.
Announcing new versions of tools for social media analysis.

recommendations: Diversify content formats to include videos and infographics to increase engagement.
Use platform-specific hashtags to improve content discoverability.
Engage with followers by asking questions and responding to comments to foster community.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is used to share research papers and promote conference presentations, achieving moderate engagement through likes and reposts.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon is used for sharing updates on tool releases and discussing methodological approaches, with a relatively lower level of engagement compared to other platforms.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn shows the highest engagement, particularly regarding announcements of research grants and job openings, suggesting a strong professional network.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
